description_long: This object is a tall, metallic lattice structure with a wide base anchored to a concrete platform, tapering as it rises. At the top, it features a prominent horizontal crossarm with three insulator stacks hanging from each side, designed to support high-voltage power lines. The lattice framework is constructed from intersecting steel beams, providing both strength and stability. The structure is predominantly gray, with a matte metallic finish, and the insulators are small, dark, and cylindrical. There are no visible warning signs or mounted electrical equipment beyond the insulators. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for supporting and distributing electrical power lines in urban environments.
description: A tall, gray metallic lattice tower with a wide base and a horizontal crossarm supporting insulator stacks for power lines. The structure is anchored to a concrete platform and features intersecting steel beams.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the crossarm with three insulator stacks on each side and the lattice structure tapering upward from a wide base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the narrow profile of the tower, with the crossarm appearing as a thin line and the lattice framework visible in depth.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the crossarm and insulator stacks, as well as the symmetrical lattice structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the narrow profile and the depth of the lattice framework.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted at the top, extending symmetrically on both sides with insulator stacks hanging below each end.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: The power lines are configured in two parallel circuits, each with three-phase arrangement, routed horizontally and symmetrically from both sides of the crossarm.
